SPLASH TRAINING GUIDE

Contest Vault

Collecting entry fees and paying out winners, powered by LeagueSafe

01
OVERVIEW

What Contest Vault Is

Contest Vault is Splash's built-in, invite-only way to collect entry fees and pay out winners, powered by a direct integration with LeagueSafe. Entry fees are held securely until the contest is finalized, then winners get paid out — no manual collection, no chasing down Venmo requests.

Note

Before Contest Vault can go live, the commissioner needs a LeagueSafe account. If they don't already have one, they'll create it as part of turning Contest Vault on — the same account creation screen entrants use to pay their entry fee (see Section 05).

  • All 50 states + Canada — no geolocation required to pay in
  • You're still in control — your contest, your rules, your way
  • Save hours every contest — LeagueSafe handles collection, payouts, and the chase-down
  • Hybrid-friendly — collect online or log cash/Venmo payments, all tracked in one vault
  • Splash takes $0 — no platform fee, just a standard 3% payment processing fee
Tip

You can enable Contest Vault anytime after creating your contest — it isn't required at setup, and turning it on later doesn't affect entrants who've already joined.

Note

The product copy also mentions automatic refunds if a contest is cancelled or a participant is removed. That's stated in-app, but hasn't yet been confirmed against the actual settings/transactions screens — treat it as likely, not guaranteed, until we can verify it against product behavior directly.

✏️ Quick Check

1. What percentage does LeagueSafe keep for payment processing on Contest Vault payments?

2. Does Splash take a cut of entry fees collected through Contest Vault?

02
SETUP

Turning On Contest Vault

Contest Vault shows up as step 2 of the commissioner's onboarding checklist on the Dashboard, right after the contest is created. A commissioner can set it up immediately, or choose to manage payments themselves and turn it on later.

Note

Clicking "Set up Contest Vault" here redirects the commissioner to log in or create a LeagueSafe account — linking Splash and LeagueSafe for this contest. It's the same screen entrants see in Section 05 when they pay their entry fee.

Contest Vault is also reachable anytime afterward — not just during onboarding:

Entry pointWhere to find it
Dashboard checklist"Set up entry fee collection" → Set up Contest Vault
Commissioner Dashboard gridContest Vault card (shows On/Off status)
Left navContest Vault (Commissioner group)
Contest Settings → Entry fees"Vault settings" button
Note

The Entry fees tab shows a shortcut labeled "Configure full payment settings in the Payment Vault," with a "Vault settings" button. This opens the same Contest Vault settings screen — it's a wording inconsistency in that one banner, not a separate feature.

Warn

Once a commissioner opens Contest Vault settings but hasn't finished configuring them, the status reads "Pending confirmation" rather than "On" or "Off." Entry fee and payout settings aren't active until "Confirm settings" is clicked.

✏️ Quick Check

1. Which of these is NOT a way to reach Contest Vault settings?

2. What does a "Pending confirmation" status mean?

↑ Back to top
03
PAYOUTS

Payout Rule & Voting

Inside Contest Vault → Settings → General, the commissioner sets a Payout rule that controls how prize money gets approved before it's distributed. There are two options: Vote or Automatic.

With Vote selected, the product describes the rule exactly like this: league funds are distributed only after a set percentage of league members vote to approve the commissioner's allocation, and no funds will be distributed until that approval is reached — no exceptions.

Vote typeWhat it takes to approve a payout
Majority approval51% of league members vote yes
Unanimous approvalEvery league member votes yes
Warn

"Automatic" removes the vote requirement — it does not mean payouts send themselves. A commissioner still has to manually distribute funds; see Section 04 for that distinction.

✏️ Quick Check

1. Under Majority approval, what percentage of league members must approve a payout?

2. If a commissioner sets the Payout rule to Automatic, do league members need to vote before funds move?

↑ Back to top
04
SETTINGS

Deadlines, Late Fees & Payout Structure

The rest of General settings covers when entry fee payments are due, what happens if someone pays late, and how the prize pool itself gets split up.

Entry fee payments can't be made after the selected deadline. Before that date, an entrant can withdraw their own entry and get refunded. After the deadline passes, a refund only happens if the commissioner manually removes that entrant from the contest.

Note

When "Allow late payments" is on, the late fee is added on top of the standard entry fee for anyone who pays after the deadline.

Payout structure can be set either as percentages of the prize pool or as flat dollar amounts. Commissioners can configure standard place-based payouts (1st, 2nd, 3rd, etc.) or fully custom payouts — for example, a weekly prize alongside a season-long one.

Warn

Payout structure only defines the plan — it does not distribute anything by itself. The commissioner still needs to manually distribute payments from the Contest Vault Dashboard tab, whether the Payout rule is Vote or Automatic.

✏️ Quick Check

1. True or false: with Payout rule set to Automatic, payouts go out with zero action from the commissioner.

2. Which payment deadline option is NOT available?

↑ Back to top
05
ENTRANTS

Paying Your Entry Fee

When an entrant is ready to pay, they're taken to a payment screen owned and hosted by LeagueSafe — not Splash. First-time payers create a LeagueSafe account as part of the flow; returning payers just log in.

Note

See Section 02 for a screenshot of this LeagueSafe login/account-creation screen — it's the same one shown there for the commissioner.

  • Entrants can log in with Facebook, Google, or Apple, or with an email and password
  • New entrants can create a LeagueSafe account from the same screen
  • Identity and payments on this screen are secured by LeagueSafe, not Splash
Tip

Paying isn't required to enter a contest. An entrant can join now and pay whenever works for them, up until the payment deadline the commissioner set.

✏️ Quick Check

1. Which of the following can entrants use to log into LeagueSafe?

↑ Back to top

COMING SOON

Contest Vault Dashboard & Transactions

Contest Vault has three tabs once it's set up: Dashboard, Transactions, and Settings. This guide currently covers Settings in full — Dashboard and Transactions are still being documented.

Warn

Screenshots for the Contest Vault Dashboard and Transactions tabs are still pending. This section will be filled in with the actual payment-tracking and payout-distribution flow once they're available.

07
COMING SOON

Withdrawals & KYC

Entrants aren't required to complete identity verification (KYC) to pay an entry fee, but KYC is expected to be required before withdrawing any winnings — that detail comes from earlier product notes, not yet a screenshot of the actual withdrawal flow.

Warn

This section is a placeholder. The actual KYC and withdrawal screens haven't been captured yet — nothing here should be treated as confirmed until we can verify it against the real flow.

08
REFERENCE

Quick Reference FAQ

QuestionAnswer
Do I have to use Contest Vault?No — it's optional. Turn it on now, later, or keep managing entry fees yourself.
Can I enable and disable Contest Vault multiple times?No — once you disable it, you can't turn it back on within that same contest. To use Contest Vault again, the commissioner will need to create a new contest.
What does it cost?A flat 3% payment processing fee to LeagueSafe. Splash takes $0, and it waives the standard self-managed service fee.
Can entrants outside the US and Canada pay in?Not directly through LeagueSafe yet — but they can still pay the commissioner directly and get tracked.
What happens if someone doesn't pay by the deadline?They can no longer pay in. Before the deadline they can self-withdraw and get refunded; after it, only a commissioner-initiated removal triggers a refund.
Can payouts be percentages instead of flat dollar amounts?Yes — the commissioner chooses either Percentages or Dollar Amounts for the payout structure.
Does "Automatic" mean payouts send themselves?No — it just removes the vote requirement. The commissioner still manually distributes payments.
↑ Back to top
09
REFERENCE

Glossary

TermMeaning
Contest VaultSplash's built-in entry fee collection and payout feature, powered by LeagueSafe.
LeagueSafeSplash's payment partner — holds entry fee funds securely and hosts the actual payment screen.
Payout ruleWhether payouts require a Vote or go out Automatically (without a vote).
Vote typeHow approval is measured for a vote-based payout: Majority (51%) or Unanimous (100%).
Payment deadlineThe cutoff after which entry fee payments can no longer be submitted.
Late feeAn extra charge added to the standard entry fee for payments made after the deadline, if enabled.
Payout structureHow the prize pool is split — by Percentages or Dollar Amounts, across place-based or custom payouts.
Place payoutsStandard payout slots tied to finishing position (1st, 2nd, 3rd, etc.).
Custom payoutsPayout slots the commissioner defines beyond standard place finishes — e.g. a weekly prize.
KYCKnow Your Customer — identity verification, expected to be required before withdrawing funds.
